item.title: Trump’s ‘shock and war’ makes this economic crisis different
item.description: Conflict with Iran will leave deeper and more lasting scars than last year’s tariffs crisis

item.title: The endlessly fascinating office protected species
item.description: Being deemed close to the boss lets you get away with murder — until it doesn’t

item.title: All respect, Timothée, art is about more than popularity
item.description: Some of the outrage appears disingenuous but the actor is wrong in his dismissal of opera and ballet

item.title: Wealth taxes’ design flaws consign them to failure
item.description: It may be easier for countries sitting on large deficits to tighten the screws on existing levies

item.title: Higher oil prices are just a short-term worry
item.description: Long-term inflation expectations are staying steady

item.title: Sovereign AI is a bet on the economies of anti-scale
item.description: Deglobalisation is expensive for individual countries, but a windfall for their suppliers

item.title: Why Iran could become the next Iraq
item.description: The first Gulf war left a weakened regime in place without a political settlement — paving the way for future conflict

item.title: Are markets being too complacent about the Iran war?
item.description: Sometimes crises do not blow over as investors might hope

item.title: Donald Trump, globalist
item.description: It should not have taken the Iran war to finish the idea that he is ‘isolationist’

item.title: The Noma affair will leave a very nasty taste in the mouth
item.description: High-end restaurants as a force for moral good was an idea long lacking in self-awareness

item.title: Pinterest’s mood board should include a potential sale
item.description: Many of its users may gather ideas on the platform but shop somewhere else

item.title: AI is dressing up greed as progress on creative rights
item.description: The problem is not that the law is unfit for the 21st century but that it is being flouted

item.title: Deepfakes, wigs and the war on truth
item.description: Amid the online slop of casual deceptions, everything now requires a second look

item.title: What to do when you own more wine than you’ll ever drink
item.description: A generation of serious collectors are confronting an enviable problem: more bottles than time

item.title: What a food truck and a fine dining restaurant can tell us about life in Puerto Rico — restaurant review
item.description: Leña Eh and 1919 sit at opposite ends of San Juan’s restaurant scene

item.title: Is your Isa strategy up to date?
item.description: Upcoming changes to pensions rules will have a significant impact on how people use Isas in retirement

item.title: Under 40? Open a Lifetime Isa while you still can
item.description: A replacement Isa for first-time buyers risks losing important benefits of investing

item.title: Show me a leader who doesn’t love a crisis
item.description: Global events hide mistakes, confer agency and are exciting to boot

item.title: Iran war will further damage fragile inflation psychology
item.description: Business and households alike are worrying — the Fed should be too

item.title: How Labour should deal with the backbench rebel alliance
item.description: Their influence is not inherently problematic — but such visible challenges to key policy proposals are

item.title: Iran and the rising perils of AI in warfare
item.description: Limits on the use of lethal autonomous weapons systems are urgent

item.title: Private credit has half the ingredients of a financial crisis
item.description: Worries arise that valuations shown on balance sheets don’t match reality

item.title: Lessons from an unwise banking photo shoot
item.description: An interview with four young men in finance dressed in Armani jackets and Hermès ties exposes several tensions

item.title: Should I go to the reunion?
item.description: If you’re even halfway happy with your life choices, I say go for it

item.title: The US consumer is OK
item.description: But if oil stays expensive . . .
